Flow.Begin.description: The first shape to be executed in a Jibo Flow Editor procedure, only one allowed per procedure.

options.description: Prototype function text that is copied to provide input parms (and defaults) to this procedure when it is called from another procedure as a subflow. This code is just a text template and is not executed here.

Flow.Begin-Parallel.description: The beginning of an Asynchronous thread, more than one allowed per procedure. Execution commences when the page is invoked, simultaneously with the Flow.Begin on the page.

Flow.Picture.description: An image to display on the diagram as an annotation (not an activity that is executed at runtime).

options.description: The picture title.
The URI/Filename for the picture.
The picture caption.
The text of the comment.

Flow.End.description: Ends execution of a Jibo Flow Editor procedure and returns to the calling procedure, or exits if this is the top-most procedure.

options.description: Returns the string value that will be used as the Transition from the subflow activity (if undefined, the Flow.End activity name is used).
path to subflow's .flow file (without the .flow extension)
Returns the object that appears in the called subflow as 'notepad.params'.
Returns the transition result from the subflow.

Flow.Subtree.description: Executes a behavior tree (.bt) as a single behavior and returns the status of the subtree.

options.description: Select a subtree.
Returns an object that will be this tree's notepad.
Returns the transition result from the subtree.

Flow.Throw.description: Throws an exception (the name of the activity becomes the name of the exception thrown).

options.description: Returns an object that will be made available as the 'payload' argument to Flow.Catch.

Flow.Interrupt.description: For use in Parallel threads; causes the Primary thread to take an exception, no matter where it is (the name of the activity becomes the name of the exception thrown).

options.description: Returns an object that will be made available as the 'payload' argument to Flow.Catch.

Flow.Catch.description: Catches the exception given by the name of the activity.

options.description: Called with the actual Exception string and the return value from the Exception or Interrupt's getPayload() functions. Returns the Flow.Catch transition value.

Flow.Eval.description: Evaluates a Javascript function, the return value of the function becomes the activity result.

options.description: The return value becomes the activity result.

Flow.Eval-Async.description: Evaluates a Javascript function, the value passed to 'done' becomes the activity result.

options.description: Function to be executed. The value passed to 'done' becomes the activity result.
Function to be executed when/if the activity receives a stop request.

Flow.Wait.description: Pauses for the specified number of milliseconds.

options.description: The number of milliseconds to pause.

Mim.description: A Multimodal Interaction Module, for all your speaking needs.

options.description: Return path to mim file (from project root)
Get Prompt Data - Return variables for Mim prompt conditions and text replacement
MIM result - allows modification of an individual listen result before the MIM analyzes it
MIM Success - provides data on the end result of the MIM - a successful response.
MIM Failure - provides data on the failure of the MIM - an overall failure due to no input or no matching response.

Mim.Announcement.description: A Multimodal Interaction Module that plays announcements.

options.description: path to mim file (from project root)
Get Prompt Data - Return variables for Mim prompt conditions and text replacement

Mim.Optional-Response.description: A Multimodal Interaction Module that plays an Announcement and waits briefly for a response.

options.description: path to mim file (from project root)
Get Prompt Data - Return variables for Mim prompt conditions and text replacement
MIM result - allows modification of an individual listen result before the MIM analyzes it
MIM Success - provides data on the end result of the MIM - a successful response.

Mim.Question.description: A Multimodal Interaction Module that plays an Announcement and waits indefinitely for a response.

options.description: path to mim file (from project root)
Get Prompt Data - Return variables for Mim prompt conditions and text replacement
MIM result - allows modification of an individual listen result before the MIM analyzes it
MIM Success - provides data on the end result of the MIM - a successful response.
MIM Failure - provides data on the failure of the MIM - an overall failure due to no input or no matching response.

Menu.description: A self contained single stage menu.

options.description: Provide the menu config data.
Called when the menu is closed by the user or a timeout.
Called when a user utterance matches the menu rule, or an item is selected
Called when a user tries to choose an item with positional selection - 'open the second one', etc
